edges.description: Autophagic-lysosomal failure leads to accumulation of lipids and material in lysosomes.
edges.source: TRPML1 Channel Dysfunction and Autophagic-Lysosomal Failure

edges.target: TRPML1 Channel Dysfunction and Autophagic-Lysosomal Failure

nodes.description: Impaired lysosomal degradation and trafficking cause accumulation of lipids and other material in lysosomes throughout the nervous system and other tissues, driving a hypomyelinating leukodystrophy with severe psychomotor delay and progressive visual loss.

evidence.snippet: Mucolipidosis IV (MLIV) is an ultra-rare lysosomal storage disorder characterized by severe psychomotor delay, progressive visual impairment, and achlorhydria.

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function MCOLN1 variants abolishing TRPML1

evidence.snippet: Mucolipidosis IV (MLIV) is an ultra-rare lysosomal storage disorder characterized by severe psychomotor delay, progressive visual impairment, and achlorhydria.

nodes.description: Biallelic MCOLN1 variants abolish the endolysosomal TRPML1 cation channel. Loss of TRPML1-mediated lysosomal calcium release impairs autophagy, lysosomal membrane trafficking, and lysosomal reformation, the primary cellular lesion of MLIV.
nodes.id: TRPML1 Channel Dysfunction and Autophagic-Lysosomal Failure

evidence.reference_title: MCOLN1/TRPML1 in the lysosome: a promising target for autophagy modulation in diverse diseases.
